title: Epitaph I. On Charles Earl Of Dorset, In The Church Of Withyam, In Sussex.
content:
'His saltem accumulem donis, et fungar inani Munere!'

    VIRG.

    Dorset, the grace of courts, the Muses' pride,
    Patron of arts, and judge of nature, died.
    The scourge of pride, though sanctified or great,
    Of fops in learning, and of knaves in state:
    Yet soft his nature, though severe his lay,
    His anger moral, and his wisdom gay.
    Bless'd satirist! who touch'd the mean so true,
    As show'd vice had his hate and pity too.
    Blest courtier! who could king and country please,
    Yet sacred keep his friendships, and his ease.
    Blest peer! his great forefathers' every grace
    Reflecting, and reflected in his race;
    Where other Buckhursts, other Dorsets shine,
    And patriots still, or poets, deck the line.
meaning:
"Epitaph I. On Charles Earl Of Dorset, In The Church Of Withyam, In Sussex." is a quintessential example of Alexander Pope's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
